forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,20 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: a580
+project: local-review
+title: 'Review: delete dead .hashes incremental tracking'
+---
+`.validators/.hashes/` skip-hash only fires for `Scope::Working`, but `/finish` commits each checkpoint and reviews `sha HEAD~1..HEAD` — so the working scope is never used and the hash store is never written (the "empty after 54 sessions" report; structural, not a bug). A prior task (`01KVJV852P5SXCYD7NB99C5G4J`) already burned a cycle chasing a phantom path-normalization bug inside this dead module.
+
+## Remove
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/tracking.rs` — delete the module (~1000 lines): `TrackingEntry`, `record_baseline_if_working`, `subtract_unchanged`, `ensure_gitignore`, `rel_key`/`clean_relative`, all of it.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs`: drop the `use_tracking`/`rules_hash` params and the subtract-unchanged filter from `resolve_working` (`:540-597`); `scope_review` loses the `use_tracking` arg.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/synthesize.rs::run_review` + `drive.rs::run_review_over_agent`: drop the `use_tracking` param and the baseline-record tail.
+- `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/review_op.rs`: drop `ReviewRequest.force` and the `use_tracking = !force` plumbing.
+- Review tool/skill: remove the `force`/`--force`/`all` escape hatch surface and its docs.
+- Delete the tracking + incremental-tracking tests in scope.rs/drive.rs/tracking.rs.
+
+## Verify
+`cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green; no `.validators/.hashes/` or `.gitignore` writing remains anywhere.
\ No newline at end of file
reverse_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -1,20 +0,0 @@
----
-assignees:
-- claude-code
-position_column: todo
-position_ordinal: a580
-project: local-review
-title: 'Review: delete dead .hashes incremental tracking'
----
-`.validators/.hashes/` skip-hash only fires for `Scope::Working`, but `/finish` commits each checkpoint and reviews `sha HEAD~1..HEAD` — so the working scope is never used and the hash store is never written (the "empty after 54 sessions" report; structural, not a bug). A prior task (`01KVJV852P5SXCYD7NB99C5G4J`) already burned a cycle chasing a phantom path-normalization bug inside this dead module.
-
-## Remove
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/tracking.rs` — delete the module (~1000 lines): `TrackingEntry`, `record_baseline_if_working`, `subtract_unchanged`, `ensure_gitignore`, `rel_key`/`clean_relative`, all of it.
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs`: drop the `use_tracking`/`rules_hash` params and the subtract-unchanged filter from `resolve_working` (`:540-597`); `scope_review` loses the `use_tracking` arg.
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/synthesize.rs::run_review` + `drive.rs::run_review_over_agent`: drop the `use_tracking` param and the baseline-record tail.
-- `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/review_op.rs`: drop `ReviewRequest.force` and the `use_tracking = !force` plumbing.
-- Review tool/skill: remove the `force`/`--force`/`all` escape hatch surface and its docs.
-- Delete the tracking + incremental-tracking tests in scope.rs/drive.rs/tracking.rs.
-
-## Verify
-`cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green; no `.validators/.hashes/` or `.gitignore` writing remains anywhere.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -50,4 +50,11 @@
 - Delete the tracking + incremental-tracking tests in scope.rs/drive.rs/tracking.rs.

 ## Verify
-`cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green; no `.validators/.hashes/` or `.gitignore` writing remains anywhere.
\ No newline at end of file
+`cargo test -p swissarmyhammer-validators` green; no `.validators/.hashes/` or `.gitignore` writing remains anywhere.
+
+## Review Findings (2026-06-26 22:51)
+
+### Warnings
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-agent/tests/review_real_model_e2e.rs:42` — Six error-pattern strings are hardcoded in an OR chain (m.contains("429") || m.contains("too many requests") || ...). Each check does the same operation on different literals. This exceeds the rule of three and should be expressed as data. Extract the patterns to a constant array: `const MODEL_UNAVAILABLE_PATTERNS: &[&str] = &["429", "too many requests", "rate limited", "loadingfailed", "failed to load", "model loading failed"];` and iterate with `.iter().any(|p| m.contains(p))`.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/mod.rs:266` — The error message at the end of the match statement hardcodes the list of valid operations ('review file', 'review working', 'review sha', 'list validators', 'get validator', 'check validators'). This duplicates the REVIEW_OPERATIONS vector defined elsewhere in the same file. When a new operation is added to REVIEW_OPERATIONS, this error message must be manually updated or it will diverge. Generate the operation list dynamically from REVIEW_OPERATIONS in the error message, e.g., `REVIEW_OPERATIONS.iter().map(|op| format!("'{}'", op.op_string())).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(", ")`.
+- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:145` — The `scope_review` function contains approximately 65+ lines of actual code (excluding blanks and pure comments), exceeding the 50-line threshold. The function handles multiple distinct responsibilities: resolving scope files, computing semantic diffs, grouping entities by file, matching validators to files, running probes, computing per-file facts, and assembling the final work list. These should be split into smaller, focused functions. Extract the work-list assembly stages into separate helper functions, such as `group_entities_by_file()`, `match_validators_and_files()`, `compute_per_file_facts()`, and `assemble_validator_work()`. This will improve readability, testability, and maintainability.
\ No newline at end of file

Extract the patterns to a constant array: `const MODEL_UNAVAILABLE_PATTERNS: &[&str] = &["429", "too many requests", "rate limited", "loadingfailed", "failed to load", "model loading failed"];` and iterate with `.iter().any(|p| m.contains(p))`.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/mod.rs:266` — The error message at the end of the match statement hardcodes the list of valid operations ('review file', 'review working', 'review sha', 'list validators', 'get validator', 'check validators'). This duplicates the REVIEW_OPERATIONS vector defined elsewhere in the same file. When a new operation is added to REVIEW_OPERATIONS, this error message must be manually updated or it will diverge. Generate the operation list dynamically from REVIEW_OPERATIONS in the error message, e.g., `REVIEW_OPERATIONS.iter().map(|op| format!("'{}'", op.op_string())).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(", ")`.
-- [ ] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:145` — The `scope_review` function contains approximately 65+ lines of actual code (excluding blanks and pure comments), exceeding the 50-line threshold. The function handles multiple distinct responsibilities: resolving scope files, computing semantic diffs, grouping entities by file, matching validators to files, running probes, computing per-file facts, and assembling the final work list. Each check does the same operation on different literals. This exceeds the rule of three and should be expressed as data. Extract the patterns to a constant array: `const MODEL_UNAVAILABLE_PATTERNS: &[&str] = &["429", "too many requests", "rate limited", "loadingfailed", "failed to load", "model loading failed"];` and iterate with `.iter().any(|p| m.contains(p))`.
-- [x] `crates/swissarmyhammer-tools/src/mcp/tools/review/mod.rs:266` — The error message at the end of the match statement hardcodes the list of valid operations ('review file', 'review working', 'review sha', 'list validators', 'get validator', 'check validators'). This duplicates the REVIEW_OPERATIONS vector defined elsewhere in the same file. When a new operation is added to REVIEW_OPERATIONS, this error message must be manually updated or it will diverge. Generate the operation list dynamically from REVIEW_OPERATIONS in the error message, e.g., `REVIEW_OPERATIONS.iter().map(|op| format!("'{}'", op.op_string())).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(", ")`.
-- [x] `crates/swissarmyhammer-validators/src/review/scope.rs:145` — The `scope_review` function contains approximately 65+ lines of actual code (excluding blanks and pure comments), exceeding the 50-line threshold. The function handles multiple distinct responsibilities: resolving scope files, computing semantic diffs, grouping entities by file, matching validators to files, running probes, computing per-file facts, and assembling the final work list. These should be split into smaller, focused functions. Extract the work-list assembly stages into separate helper functions, such as `group_entities_by_file()`, `match_validators_and_files()`, `compute_per_file_facts()`, and `assemble_validator_work()`. This will improve readability, testability, and maintainability.
\ No newline at end of file

+- actor: claude-code

+  text: |-

+
+    Finding 1 — review_real_model_e2e.rs: extracted the six OR-chained substrings in is_model_unavailable into `const MODEL_UNAVAILABLE_PATTERNS: &[&str]` and replaced the chain with `MODEL_UNAVAILABLE_PATTERNS.iter().any(|p| m.contains(p))`. Verified live by the real-model e2e (review_runs_over_acp_against_a_real_local_model) passing in the rdeps run.
+
+    Finding 2 — review/mod.rs: the unknown-op match arm now derives the valid-op list from REVIEW_OPERATIONS via `REVIEW_OPERATIONS.iter().map(|op| format!("'{}'", op.op_string())).collect::<Vec<_>>().join(", ")` instead of the hardcoded literal list. op_string() already exists on the Operation trait. Order/format identical to the old literal, so the message text is unchanged (no test churn).
+
+    Finding 3 — review/scope.rs: split scope_review's inline work-list assembly into four focused helpers — group_entities_by_file (-> GroupedEntities), match_validators_and_files (-> MatchedValidators), compute_per_file_facts, assemble_validator_work. scope_review now reads as a sequence of named steps (resolve -> diff -> group -> match -> probes -> per-file facts -> assemble). Behavior identical (same sorting, same dedup, same probe-cache filter).
